classes.purpose: Understand and organize an advisory engagement
classes.content: Client and project labels; objectives and audience; company, participant and stakeholder identities; source documents, decks, spreadsheets, notes, transcripts, recordings, dates, facts, financial or operating data, constraints, risks, and open questions

classes.purpose: Weigh evidence and prepare decision-ready advice
classes.content: Material summaries, evidence maps, source limitations, consultant and advisor judgement, Codex inferences, contradictions, hypotheses, options, recommendations, implementation conditions, pending inclusion decisions, and case issues

classes.purpose: Draft and review workpapers, briefs, decks, memos, and decision packs
classes.content: Narrative, charts, claims, citations, speaker notes, review findings, approved content, and final deliverables

boundaries_beyond_codex.destination: Public or otherwise authorized external sources selected for the engagement
boundaries_beyond_codex.purpose: Obtain current industry, market, governance, legal, or other external context relevant to the advisory question
boundaries_beyond_codex.content: Research questions, search terms, selected URLs, retrieved public material, and concise source takeaways; queries may reflect engagement facts when Codex uses them
controls: Use public or authorized sources only.
Do not copy proprietary case material into a public research source or shared playbook.

boundaries_beyond_codex.destination: Mparanza Hosted Voice and its configured realtime/transcription provider
boundaries_beyond_codex.purpose: Capture or upload a spoken debrief and return a transcript bundle
boundaries_beyond_codex.content: Compact case context when enabled, live or uploaded recording content, source metadata, transcript, and media metadata
controls: Hosted Voice is used only after the user selects a voice capture or upload route.
The separate hosted-service record states the source-backed access and retention position.

boundaries_beyond_codex.purpose: Transmit a technical problem or improvement suggestion to the developer and check its status
boundaries_beyond_codex.content: The exact sanitized request approved by the user, plugin name and version, request kind, idempotency metadata, and later receipt identifiers and status tokens
controls: The Clara skill instructs Codex to show and sanitize the exact submission before invoking the helper; the helper itself accepts arbitrary bounded JSON.
The skill instructs Codex to exclude client, source, case, credential, secret, and identifying material.
The skill requires separate consent for problem reports and suggestions; the command-line helper cannot prove that consent occurred.

boundaries_beyond_codex.purpose: Notify the user when a newer Clara version is published
boundaries_beyond_codex.content: A GET request with a fixed update-check User-Agent; no case files, prompts, transcripts, or client content are included by the plugin
controls: The request uses a fixed HTTPS manifest URL and a three-second timeout.
The check is rate-limited locally and fails open without blocking Clara.
